description: Evaluates the step creator helper skill for generating workflow steps with all 12 mandatory fields, covering mechanical steps, LLM steps, handoff steps, and quality bar adherence.

evals.name: standard-llm-step
evals.prompt: Generate step 3 of a content moderation workflow: 'Classify User Input'. The workflow objective is to analyze user-submitted content for policy violations. Previous steps: 1. Receive Input, 2. Sanitize Input.
evals.expected_output: A step with all 12 fields populated, including a full LLM prompt with placeholders for classification, specific validation rules, and concrete failure/recovery actions.
expectations: All 12 fields present: stepNumber, title, desc, whyThisMatters, inputNeeded, actionInstruction, promptToUse, expectedOutput, validationRule, failureSignal, recoveryAction, handoffIfNeeded
promptToUse contains a full LLM prompt with {{placeholders}} (not just a description)
whyThisMatters explains business rationale (not a restatement of title)
validationRule is testable (e.g., 'result.category IN [safe, warning, violation]')
failureSignal is observable (e.g., specific error types, timeouts)
recoveryAction is concrete (not just 'try again')

evals.name: mechanical-step-no-llm
evals.prompt: Generate step 1 of an API validation workflow: 'Validate Request Schema'. This is a mechanical step, no LLM involved. Workflow objective: validate incoming API requests against OpenAPI schema.
evals.expected_output: A step with promptToUse set to 'null (mechanical step)' but all other 11 fields fully populated with concrete values.
expectations: promptToUse is explicitly 'null (mechanical step)'
All other 11 fields are fully populated (not skipped because it's mechanical)
actionInstruction references specific validation library or function call
failureSignal includes infrastructure failures even though the step 'always succeeds' logically
inputNeeded specifies data types (e.g., 'requestBody: JSON, schema: OpenAPISpec')
expectedOutput describes specific success output format

evals.name: step-with-handoff
evals.prompt: Generate step 5 of a security review workflow: 'Escalate Critical Finding'. When severity is critical, hand off to the security-agent. Workflow objective: review code changes for security vulnerabilities.
evals.expected_output: A step with handoffIfNeeded containing decision logic for routing to different agents based on severity levels.
expectations: handoffIfNeeded contains decision logic (e.g., 'If severity=critical -> security-agent; if medium -> review-agent; else continue')
handoffIfNeeded is not just a single agent ID but includes routing conditions
validationRule covers the handoff decision criteria
failureSignal includes 'handoff target unavailable' scenario
recoveryAction addresses what happens if the handoff fails

evals.name: quality-bar-bad-fields
evals.prompt: Generate step 2 of an agent loading workflow: 'Load Agent Configuration'. Workflow context: multi-agent orchestration system that loads and validates agent definitions.
evals.expected_output: Step follows the GOOD column quality bar, not the BAD column. Each field has specific, implementable content.
expectations: actionInstruction follows GOOD pattern (e.g., 'Call loader.loadAgent(agentId), destructure response') not BAD ('Load the agent')
validationRule follows GOOD pattern (e.g., 'response.fields.length === 21') not BAD ('Check it works')
failureSignal follows GOOD pattern (e.g., 'loadAgent throws AgentNotFoundError') not BAD ('It fails')
recoveryAction follows GOOD pattern (e.g., 'Load default template, log warning') not BAD ('Try again')
whyThisMatters justifies the step's existence, not restates the title

evals.name: always-succeeds-step
evals.prompt: Generate step 4 of a logging workflow: 'Write Audit Log Entry'. This step writes to an append-only audit log and should always succeed under normal conditions. Workflow objective: maintain compliance audit trail.
evals.expected_output: Even though the step 'always succeeds', failureSignal still covers infrastructure failures (timeout, disk full, network error).
expectations: failureSignal includes infrastructure failures: timeout, out of memory, disk full, network error
Does not set failureSignal to 'N/A' or 'none' even though the step logically always succeeds
recoveryAction addresses infrastructure failure scenarios (e.g., 'retry with exponential backoff, write to fallback log')
validationRule verifies the log entry was written (e.g., 'response.status === 201 OR entry appears in log within 5s')
whyThisMatters explains compliance rationale for audit logging
